validation_summary.evidence: python -m py_compile on both new files exits 0 (0.13s); python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_merge_batch passes 16/16 (12.0s); python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests passes 134/134 up from 118 (21.6s)

checkpoint.summary: unit_020 complete. New skills/development-harness/scripts/merge_batch.py (stdlib-only, 217 LOC) does the serial fan-in merge of a dispatched batch: fleet.mode -> 'merging', captures pre_merge_ref, iterates fleet.units running 'git merge --no-ff' per unit with configurable conflict_strategy. abort_batch flips the conflicted unit to 'failed' and skips remaining units (also 'failed', conflict=null); serialize_conflicted leaves the conflicted unit status='running' with conflict recorded so it stays alive for a later batch. Post-merge runs an injected run_post_merge_validation callable (default no-op); on failure, 'git reset --hard <pre_merge_ref>' rolls back and every merged unit is downgraded to 'failed' with strategy_applied='post_merge_validation_failed'. On success, merged units' worktrees + branches are cleaned up and fleet.mode -> 'idle'. 16 new tests across 7 classes cover all outcomes: happy path, abort_batch, serialize_conflicted, validation failure with HEAD verification, validator-not-called-when-nothing-merged, unknown strategy raises, and one end-to-end case that runs dispatch_batch + commit in worktree + merge_batch. Test suite 118 -> 134.
checkpoint.next_action: Complete unit_021 (PHASE_005): new skills/development-harness/scripts/teardown_batch.py -- idempotent cleanup of worktrees under .harness/worktrees/ and branches matching 'harness/batch_*/'. Callable from /clear, /sync, and error recovery paths; second run must be a no-op; handles missing worktrees/branches gracefully.
